title: Classic Fluffy Pancakes
author: Family
description: Light, fluffy pancakes that come together with pantry staples. A reliable weekend favorite.
steps: Whisk together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt in a large bowl.
Make a well in the center and pour in the milk, egg, and melted butter. Mix until just combined — a few lumps are fine.
Heat a lightly oiled griddle or pan over medium-high heat.
Pour about 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on top, then flip and cook until golden.
Serve warm with syrup, butter, or fresh fruit.
notes: Don't overmix the batter — that's the secret to fluffy pancakes.

title: Lemon Garlic Roast Chicken
author: Family
description: A whole roast chicken with bright lemon and garlic, crispy skin, and juicy meat. Sunday-dinner classic.
ingredients: A few sprigs fresh thyme or rosemary
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Pat the chicken dry all over.
Rub the softened butter and olive oil over the skin. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Stuff the cavity with the lemon halves, smashed garlic, and herbs.
Place breast-side up in a roasting pan and roast for about 1 hour 15 minutes, until the juices run clear and a thermometer reads 165°F (74°C) in the thigh.
Let rest 10 minutes before carving.
notes: Save the bones to make stock for the chicken noodle soup!

title: Chicken Noodle Soup
author: Family
description: Cozy, brothy chicken noodle soup that cures whatever ails you.
ingredients: 1 tsp salt, plus more to taste
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery and cook until softened, about 6 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Add the egg noodles and simmer until tender, about 8 minutes.
Stir in the shredded chicken, season with salt, and warm through. Finish with fresh parsley.
notes: Cook the noodles separately if you're saving leftovers — they soak up broth as they sit.

title: Weeknight Beef Chili
author: Family
description: A hearty, mildly spiced beef chili that comes together fast on a busy night.
steps: Brown the ground beef in a large pot over medium-high heat, breaking it up as it cooks. Drain excess fat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the chili powder and cumin and cook 1 minute.
Add the crushed tomatoes, beans, and broth. Bring to a simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season with salt and pepper.
notes: Top with cheese, sour cream, or diced onion. Even better the next day.

title: Garden Veggie Stir-Fry
author: Family
description: A quick, colorful vegetable stir-fry with a savory garlic-soy sauce. Serve over rice.
steps: Whisk together the soy sauce, honey, and sesame oil; set aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat.
Add the carrots and broccoli and stir-fry 3 minutes.
Add the bell pepper, snap peas, and garlic and stir-fry 3 more minutes until crisp-tender.
Pour in the sauce and toss to coat for 1 minute. Serve over rice.
notes: Add tofu or leftover chicken to make it a heartier meal.
